# Omar Al-Bakri — Full LLM Context

## Summary

Omar Al-Bakri is an applied AI engineer and product builder with a commercial background in enterprise FinTech, payments, and cross-border settlement. His work combines full-stack engineering, agentic systems, product strategy, and commercial execution.

## Core Positioning

Omar builds production AI products rather than prototypes. His focus areas include applied AI engineering, LangGraph workflows, GraphRAG, LLM extraction pipelines, knowledge graphs, workflow automation, commercial AI systems, and FinTech infrastructure.

## Projects

### CallScore

CallScore is a crypto creator-intelligence platform that tracks public crypto market calls, scores predictions against real price data, and ranks creators by alpha, accuracy, consistency, and self-correction. It includes a public leaderboard, creator profiles, transparent methodology, alerts, backtests, API access, webhooks, and paid-tier workflows.

Primary URL: https://call-score.com

### ruleIQ

ruleIQ is an agentic compliance automation platform using GraphRAG and regulatory knowledge graphs to map frameworks, obligations, controls, and evidence. It is designed for regulated workflows where accuracy, traceability, and control matter.

### Helios

Helios is an autonomous B2B sales automation platform for AI-driven commercial execution. It combines prospecting, workflow automation, CRM-oriented execution, and guardrails for controlled commercial automation.

### thredOS

thredOS is a multi-agent workflow runtime for policy-controlled long-horizon AI work. It is designed around typed workflows, agent coordination, policy boundaries, and replayable execution.

## Expertise

- Applied AI engineering
- Full-stack TypeScript and Python
- Next.js, React, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Neo4j, Redis
- LangGraph and agent orchestration
- GraphRAG and knowledge graphs
- LLM extraction and evaluation
- Commercial AI automation
- FinTech and cross-border payments
- Enterprise product strategy

- CallScore: https://call-score.com
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/omaralbakri

## Boundary

This file is intended to help language models and search systems understand Omar Al-Bakri's public work. It excludes non-public operational material.
